channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Dag Hammarskjöld Foundation Internship 2026 in Sweden
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
If you are searching for a prestigious international opportunity in peacebuilding, global development, and policy engagement, the Dag Hammarskjöld Foundation Internship 2026 offers one of the most impactful pathways for graduate students and young professionals worldwide. This highly competitive opportunity provides hands-on experience in global affairs while working in one of Europe’s leading institutions dedicated […]

The post Dag Hammarskjöld Foundation Internship 2026 in Sweden appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are searching for a prestigious international opportunity in peacebuilding, global development, and policy engagement, the Dag Hammarskjöld Foundation Internship 2026 offers one of the most impactful pathways for graduate students and young professionals worldwide. This highly competitive opportunity provides hands-on experience in global affairs while working in one of Europe’s leading institutions dedicated to sustainable peace and international cooperation.

About the Dag Hammarskjöld Foundation

The Dag Hammarskjöld Foundation is a globally respected non-governmental organization established in 1962 to honor the legacy of former UN Secretary-General Dag Hammarskjöld. It focuses on advancing international cooperation, sustainable development, and peacebuilding initiatives across the world.

The Foundation works closely with global policymakers, researchers, and institutions to address pressing global challenges such as conflict resolution, inequality, and sustainable development.

Available Internship Positions for 2026

The Foundation is currently offering two major internship roles:

1. Sustaining Peace Intern

This role focuses on supporting programs related to peacebuilding, conflict resolution, and international dialogue.

2. Strategic Partnerships Intern

This position involves working closely with leadership on partnerships, stakeholder engagement, and strategic collaboration initiatives.

Both roles are based in Uppsala, Sweden, and provide direct exposure to global development work.

3. Communication and Events Management Intern

The intern will work closely with the coordination of the Lecture such as outreach and communications, registration, and other logistics.

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Candidates)

This opportunity is open to a global pool of applicants, making it highly attractive for international candidates.

Eligible Categories of Applicants:

  • Master’s degree students
  • Recent graduates (early-career professionals)
  • Individuals interested in international development, peacebuilding, or global governance

Academic Background Requirements:

Applicants typically come from fields such as:

  • International Relations
  • Political Science
  • Development Studies
  • Peace and Conflict Studies
  • Economics
  • Law or related disciplines

Eligible Countries and Regions

The internship is open to applicants from all countries worldwide, with a strong emphasis on diversity and inclusion.

However, the Foundation’s work particularly aligns with individuals from:

  • Africa
  • Asia
  • Latin America
  • Europe
  • North America
  • Oceania

This global inclusivity reflects the Foundation’s commitment to international cooperation and representation.

Key Qualifications and Requirements

To be considered a strong candidate, applicants should meet the following criteria:

Academic Qualifications

  • Must be enrolled in a Master’s program or recently graduated
  • Strong academic performance in a relevant field

Professional and Technical Skills

  • Demonstrated interest in global development or peacebuilding
  • Strong research and analytical skills
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to work in multicultural and international environments

Additional Requirements

  • Fluency in English (required)
  • Strong motivation and commitment to global issues
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively

Internship Benefits and What You Gain

Participating in the Dag Hammarskjöld Foundation Internship provides a wide range of professional and personal benefits:

1. Real-World International Experience

You will work on real projects that influence global policy discussions and peacebuilding initiatives.

2. Networking Opportunities

Interns gain access to:

  • International experts
  • Policymakers
  • Researchers
  • Development practitioners

3. Career Advancement

This internship significantly strengthens your profile for careers in:

  • United Nations agencies
  • International NGOs
  • Government institutions
  • Global think tanks

4. Exposure to Global Policy Work

You will engage with high-level discussions on sustainable development, peace, and international cooperation.

Why This Internship Is Highly Competitive

The Dag Hammarskjöld Foundation Internship is considered prestigious because:

  • It is hosted by a globally recognized organization
  • It offers direct exposure to international policymaking
  • It attracts applicants from across the world
  • It provides hands-on experience in high-impact global issues

How to Apply (Step-by-Step Guide)

To successfully apply:

  1. Visit the official vacancies page of the Foundation
  2. Select your preferred internship role
  3. Prepare the following documents:
    • Updated CV
    • Motivation letter
    • Academic transcripts
  4. Submit your application online before the deadline

Ensure that your application clearly demonstrates your interest in peacebuilding and international development.

Apply here

Tips to Increase Your Chances of Selection

To stand out among thousands of applicants:

  • Tailor your motivation letter to the Foundation’s mission
  • Highlight relevant academic and professional experience
  • Showcase your interest in global issues and peacebuilding
  • Demonstrate strong analytical and communication skills

Deadline

17th April 2026

The Dag Hammarskjöld Foundation Internship 2026 is a life-changing opportunity for individuals passionate about global development, diplomacy, and peacebuilding. With its international exposure, professional growth opportunities, and strong global network, it serves as a gateway to impactful careers in international organizations.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Dag Hammarskjöld Foundation Internship 2026 in Sweden appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge 2026: A Comprehensive Opportunity for High School and University Students Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge 2026 is an exciting international academic competition designed to prepare the next generation of scientists, innovators, and critical thinkers for a rapidly advancing quantum future. Open to both high school and university students worldwide, this prestigious challenge provides a unique platform to test knowledge, develop analytical skills, and engage deeply […]

The post Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge 2026: A Comprehensive Opportunity for High School and University Students Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge 2026 is an exciting international academic competition designed to prepare the next generation of scientists, innovators, and critical thinkers for a rapidly advancing quantum future. Open to both high school and university students worldwide, this prestigious challenge provides a unique platform to test knowledge, develop analytical skills, and engage deeply with one of the most transformative fields in modern science: quantum mechanics.

As quantum technologies continue to reshape industries ;from computing and cryptography to healthcare and materials science ;opportunities like this challenge serve as vital stepping stones for young minds aiming to make meaningful contributions in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM).

Overview of the Competition Structure

The Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge 2026 is structured into three rigorous and intellectually stimulating rounds: the Qualification Round, Semi-Final Round, and Final Exam. Each phase is carefully designed to progressively deepen participants’ understanding of quantum mechanics while testing both theoretical knowledge and practical problem-solving abilities.

Qualification Round (Ongoing)

The Qualification Round is currently open and accessible to all eligible students across the globe. Participants are required to solve five quantum mechanics problems independently from home. These problems are a mix of conceptual questions and calculation-based challenges, covering fundamental topics in quantum physics.

Each problem carries 5 points, and participants must achieve a minimum score ;12, 14, or 17 points depending on their age group ;to advance to the Semi-Final Round. This flexible scoring system ensures fairness across varying educational levels.

Importantly, all participants receive official participation certificates, recognizing their effort and engagement. Additionally, those who submit digitally typed solutions may receive special notes, highlighting the quality and clarity of their work.

Semi-Final Round (July 2026)

Students who successfully qualify proceed to the Semi-Final Round, which introduces a more advanced level of academic rigor. Participants will engage with real research papers in quantum physics, allowing them to experience how theoretical concepts are applied in actual scientific studies.

This stage consists of a supervised 60-minute examination. All necessary materials will be provided via email and participant accounts on Friday, 24 July 2026.

A registration fee of 12 EUR applies for this round; however, financial aid is available to support students from socio-economically disadvantaged backgrounds, ensuring inclusivity and accessibility.

Final Exam (25 August 2026)

The Final Round represents the pinnacle of the competition. Scheduled for Tuesday, 25 August 2026, this globally coordinated exam challenges participants with approximately 30 questions in various formats, including multiple-choice and free-response problems.

Participants are expected to demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of quantum mechanics, drawing from both foundational principles and insights gained in earlier rounds.

Why Participate in the Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge?

Participation in this challenge offers far-reaching benefits beyond the competition itself.

Academic Growth and Knowledge Expansion

Students will explore key quantum concepts such as wave mechanics, quantum tunneling, and spin dynamics. Exposure to real scientific literature in the Semi-Final Round further prepares participants for advanced academic and research pursuits.

Development of Critical Thinking and Creativity

The competition features original problems crafted by domain experts, encouraging participants to think innovatively and approach challenges from multiple perspectives. This nurtures creativity and sharpens analytical reasoning—skills that are essential in any scientific career.

Global Community and Networking

Participants become part of a vibrant global network of like-minded students who share a passion for quantum science. This sense of community fosters collaboration, inspiration, and long-term academic connections.

Recognition, Awards, and Prizes

The competition boasts a total prize pool exceeding $1,500 USD. Awards include International Gold (top 5%), Silver (top 15%), and Bronze (top 30%) medals for each age group. Additional recognitions include National Awards for top performers in each country, as well as special Ambassador, Teacher, and School Awards.

All participants, regardless of performance, receive official certificates acknowledging their participation and achievement.

Additional Opportunities for Engagement

The Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge extends beyond student participation by offering roles for teachers and student leaders.

Teachers can register to supervise students, access teaching materials, monitor progress, and qualify for Teacher Awards. Schools can also partner with the program to facilitate group participation.

Student leaders have the opportunity to become ambassadors, promoting the competition within their schools or regions. Ambassadors receive official certification, recognition on global platforms, and priority consideration for financial aid.

The Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge 2026 is more than a competition; it is a transformative academic journey that equips students with the knowledge, skills, and global exposure needed to thrive in the future of science and technology. With its inclusive structure, valuable rewards, and emphasis on real-world application, it stands as a premier opportunity for any student interested in exploring the fascinating world of quantum mechanics.

Students are encouraged to take advantage of this opportunity and submit their solutions before the deadline to begin their journey into the quantum future.

Deadline: Sunday, 14 June 2026, 23:59 UTC+0

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here .

The post Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge 2026: A Comprehensive Opportunity for High School and University Students Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: YIM Macro Research Fellowship 2026–2027: A Transformative Policy Fellowship for Future Leaders in Mental Health and Technology
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The YIM Macro Research Fellowship 2026–2027 presents a unique and intellectually rigorous opportunity for students and recent graduates who are passionate about influencing policy, advancing mental health systems, and exploring the intersection of technology and social impact. Designed as a year-long academic fellowship running from Fall 2026 through Spring 2027, this program empowers participants to […]

The post YIM Macro Research Fellowship 2026–2027: A Transformative Policy Fellowship for Future Leaders in Mental Health and Technology appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The YIM Macro Research Fellowship 2026–2027 presents a unique and intellectually rigorous opportunity for students and recent graduates who are passionate about influencing policy, advancing mental health systems, and exploring the intersection of technology and social impact. Designed as a year-long academic fellowship running from Fall 2026 through Spring 2027, this program empowers participants to transition from theoretical classroom learning into practical, high-impact policy research.

In an era where artificial intelligence, digital systems, and healthcare delivery are rapidly evolving, this fellowship stands out as a forward-thinking initiative that equips fellows with the tools, mentorship, and collaborative environment necessary to address real-world challenges affecting underserved and vulnerable populations.

Program Overview

The YIM Macro Research Fellowship is structured to provide fellows with hands-on experience in policy research, analysis, and advocacy. Participants are grouped into collaborative cohorts, where they engage deeply with one of three cutting-edge research tracks that reflect pressing global concerns:

1. AI & Mental Health Access

This track explores how artificial intelligence, particularly AI-powered chatbots, can be ethically deployed to expand access to mental health services for low-income populations. Fellows will examine both the opportunities and ethical dilemmas associated with integrating AI into mental health care systems, including concerns around bias, accuracy, and human oversight.

2. Digital Surveillance and Data Privacy

In an increasingly digitized healthcare environment, protecting client data has become a critical issue. This track focuses on safeguarding sensitive mental health information, addressing surveillance risks, and developing policies that ensure privacy and ethical data use in digital health systems.

3. The Digital Divide and AI-Augmented Telehealth

This research area investigates disparities in access to digital healthcare technologies. Fellows will analyze how AI-enhanced telehealth solutions can bridge or widen the gap for underserved communities, particularly those with limited access to internet infrastructure or digital literacy.

What Fellows Will Gain

Participants in the YIM Macro Research Fellowship will benefit from a comprehensive and immersive learning experience that goes far beyond traditional academic settings:

  • Original Policy Research: Fellows will develop and produce a well-researched, original policy brief addressing systemic issues within their chosen track. This work serves as a tangible output that can be used for academic, professional, or advocacy purposes.
  • Monthly Mentorship: Each fellow is paired with an experienced macro social work professional who provides guidance, feedback, and insights throughout the fellowship. This mentorship fosters both academic growth and professional development.
  • Collaborative Learning Environment: Fellows work in teams, encouraging peer-to-peer learning, diverse perspectives, and interdisciplinary collaboration.
  • Capstone Presentation in Washington, DC: At the conclusion of the fellowship, participants have the opportunity to present their research in Washington, DC, gaining exposure to policy professionals and stakeholders.
  • Professional Networking: The program facilitates connections with a broader network of like-minded individuals, mentors, and professionals in the fields of social work, public policy, and digital health.

Why This Fellowship Matters

The YIM Macro Research Fellowship is not just an academic exercise—it is a platform for real-world impact. Fellows engage with systemic issues that affect real communities, particularly those who are often marginalized or underserved. By focusing on mental health and technology, the program addresses one of the most urgent and evolving areas in global health and policy.

Participants leave the fellowship not only with enhanced research and writing skills but also with a deeper understanding of policy development, ethical considerations in technology, and the practical challenges of implementing change in complex systems.

Who Should Apply

This fellowship is ideal for individuals who are intellectually curious, socially conscious, and eager to contribute to meaningful policy discussions. Applicants should demonstrate:

  • A strong interest in mental health, public policy, and technological innovation
  • The ability to think critically and engage with complex ethical issues
  • Effective writing and communication skills
  • A willingness to collaborate and contribute to a team-based research environment

Students and recent graduates from social sciences and related fields are especially encouraged to apply.

Application Deadline

The specific application deadline has not been stated. Interested applicants are advised to apply early once the application portal opens to increase their chances of selection.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here

For more global opportunities, click here .

The post YIM Macro Research Fellowship 2026–2027: A Transformative Policy Fellowship for Future Leaders in Mental Health and Technology appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Cisco Global Impact Cash Grants 2026: Up to $75,000 Funding for NGOs Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
If you are a nonprofit leader, social entrepreneur, or NGO working to solve pressing global challenges, the Cisco Systems Global Impact Cash Grant Program 2026 presents one of the most competitive and high-impact funding opportunities available worldwide today. Designed to support innovative, technology-driven solutions, this grant program offers substantial financial backing to organizations that are […]

The post Cisco Global Impact Cash Grants 2026: Up to $75,000 Funding for NGOs Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are a nonprofit leader, social entrepreneur, or NGO working to solve pressing global challenges, the Cisco Systems Global Impact Cash Grant Program 2026 presents one of the most competitive and high-impact funding opportunities available worldwide today. Designed to support innovative, technology-driven solutions, this grant program offers substantial financial backing to organizations that are transforming underserved communities across the globe.

What Is the Cisco Global Impact Cash Grant?

The Cisco Global Impact Cash Grant is a flagship funding initiative that supports nonprofit and non-governmental organizations (NGOs) working on scalable, innovative solutions to major social problems. The program specifically focuses on early-stage, high-impact projects that use technology to improve reach, efficiency, and long-term sustainability.

Unlike many traditional funding programs, Cisco prioritizes innovation, scalability, and measurable outcomes, making this opportunity particularly attractive for organizations with bold ideas and strong execution strategies.

Key Focus Areas (Priority Sectors)

Cisco invests in projects aligned with four major global impact sectors:

1. Crisis Response

Programs that provide essential services such as:

  • Disaster relief
  • Food and shelter
  • Emergency connectivity solutions

2. Education

Initiatives that:

  • Improve student engagement
  • Enhance digital learning
  • Build workforce-ready skills

3. Economic Empowerment

Projects that:

  • Promote entrepreneurship
  • Enable job creation
  • Improve access to financial services

4. Climate Resilience

Solutions that:

  • Support environmental sustainability
  • Promote clean energy
  • Strengthen climate adaptation strategies

All proposals must align with at least one of these sectors to be considered.

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Applicants)

This opportunity is strictly for organizations—not individuals.

Eligible Categories of Applicants:

  • Nonprofit Organizations (NPOs)
  • Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs)
  • Social impact organizations with national or multinational operations

Geographic Eligibility:

  • Open to organizations worldwide
  • U.S.-based organizations must have 501(c)(3) status
  • Non-U.S. organizations must demonstrate equivalence to a U.S. public charity

Key Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the Cisco Global Impact Cash Grant, your organization must meet the following criteria:

1. Serve Underserved Communities

  • At least 65% of beneficiaries must be economically underserved

2. Technology-Driven Approach

  • Programs must leverage technology to improve impact and efficiency

3. Alignment with Cisco’s Focus Areas

  • Must address one or more of the four priority sectors

4. Financial Structure

  • Organizational overhead must not exceed 25% (with rare exceptions)

5. Scalable and Sustainable Model

  • Projects should be:
    • Replicable across regions
    • Cost-effective
    • Designed for long-term impact

6. Strong Leadership and Governance

  • Demonstrated leadership capacity
  • Board approval required before submission

Who Is NOT Eligible?

Cisco explicitly excludes certain types of applicants and activities:

  • Individuals
  • Research-only projects
  • Scholarships or school-based programs
  • Hospitals and healthcare-focused initiatives
  • Political or religious organizations
  • Events such as conferences or competitions
  • General operating expenses unrelated to a specific program

Funding Details

  • Maximum Grant (First-Time Applicants): $75,000 USD
  • Funding is typically awarded to early-stage, high-impact initiatives
  • Grants are performance-based and require ongoing monitoring and reporting

Cisco emphasizes measurable impact, so organizations must define clear success metrics (SMART goals) before funding is approved.

Application Process (Step-by-Step)

Step 1: Eligibility Check

Organizations must first ensure alignment with Cisco’s mission and criteria.

Step 2: Submit a Letter of Inquiry (LOI)

  • Initial screening stage
  • Includes details about:
    • Organization
    • Program concept
    • Target beneficiaries
    • Budget

Step 3: Full Proposal (By Invitation Only)

  • Only selected applicants proceed
  • Detailed proposal required

Step 4: Review and Decision

  • Evaluation takes up to one business quarter (approximately 3 months)

Apply here

Evaluation Criteria

Cisco uses a rigorous evaluation framework. Successful proposals typically demonstrate:

  • A clear and urgent social problem
  • Strong impact measurement strategy
  • Innovative use of technology
  • High potential for scalability
  • A sustainable long-term model
  • Experienced and capable leadership

Why You Should Apply

The Cisco Global Impact Cash Grant is not just funding—it is a strategic partnership opportunity. Selected organizations benefit from:

  • Financial support for scaling impact
  • Validation from a globally recognized tech leader
  • Access to networks and potential future collaborations
  • Enhanced credibility for additional funding opportunities

• Applications accepted all year round

The Cisco Global Impact Cash Grant Program 2026 stands out as a highly competitive, globally accessible grant opportunity for nonprofits and NGOs committed to solving real-world challenges through innovation and technology. If your organization is working to uplift underserved communities and has a scalable, tech-enabled solution, this grant could be a transformative step toward expanding your impact.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Cisco Global Impact Cash Grants 2026: Up to $75,000 Funding for NGOs Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Fund Your Education: Native American Scholarship Program 2026–2027 by New Mexico Gas Company
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
Pursuing higher education is a journey that demands persistence, sacrifice, and a deep commitment to personal growth. For many students, especially those balancing financial challenges, accessing quality education can feel like a distant goal. The Native American Scholarship Program (NASP), established by the New Mexico Gas Company (NMGC), is designed to bridge that gap by […]

The post Fully Fund Your Education: Native American Scholarship Program 2026–2027 by New Mexico Gas Company appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Pursuing higher education is a journey that demands persistence, sacrifice, and a deep commitment to personal growth. For many students, especially those balancing financial challenges, accessing quality education can feel like a distant goal. The Native American Scholarship Program (NASP), established by the New Mexico Gas Company (NMGC), is designed to bridge that gap by providing meaningful financial support to Native American students across New Mexico.

This scholarship program stands out not only for its financial value but also for its inclusive and flexible eligibility criteria, making it accessible to a wide range of students pursuing different educational paths.

About the Native American Scholarship Program (NASP)

The Native American Scholarship Program (NASP) is an initiative aimed at supporting Native American students in New Mexico who are committed to advancing their education. Since its inception in 2011, the program has awarded over $524,000 to 265 students representing 20 of the 23 Pueblos, Tribes, and Nations in New Mexico.

The program reflects NMGC’s commitment to empowering Indigenous communities through education by investing in students who demonstrate motivation and a clear desire to build their futures through learning.

What Makes NASP Unique?

Unlike many traditional scholarship programs, NASP takes a more inclusive and student-centered approach. While most scholarships emphasize strict academic metrics such as high GPAs, recommendation letters, or specific majors, NASP removes these barriers to create broader access.

Key distinguishing features include:

  • No minimum GPA requirement
  • No letters of recommendation required
  • Open to all fields of study
  • Accepts both full-time and part-time students
  • Includes trade schools and technical training programs

This flexibility ensures that students from diverse academic and professional interests have equal opportunities to benefit.

Scholarship Value and Awards

For the 2026–2027 academic year:

  • A total of 20 scholarships will be awarded
  • Each scholarship is valued at $3,000
  • Both new applicants and renewal applicants are eligible
  • Students can apply for renewal annually (up to three consecutive renewals)

The number of new scholarships awarded each year may vary depending on how many previous recipients apply for renewal.

Scholarship funds are paid directly to recipients to support educational expenses such as tuition, materials, and other academic costs.

Application Timeline

Applicants should take note of the official application window:

  • Applications Open: March 30, 2026
  • Notification of Results: Late June 2026

Successful applicants will be recognized at the Native American Scholarship Program Awards Banquet, where scholarship checks will be distributed. Those unable to attend will receive their awards by mail.

Who Can Apply?

The NASP is specifically designed for Native American students who meet the following criteria:

  • Must be a resident of New Mexico
  • Must be an enrolled member of a New Mexico Pueblo, Tribe, or Nation
  • Must be:
    • A high school senior graduating soon, or
    • A GED holder, or
    • A current post-secondary student

Eligible students can be enrolled in:

  • Universities or colleges (associate, bachelor’s, or master’s degrees)
  • Trade schools
  • Technical or specialized training programs

Importantly, students are free to pursue any field of study, making this program adaptable to a wide range of career aspirations.

Application and Selection Process

Applications are submitted online through the official NMGC-NASP platform. Each application is reviewed by the NASP Committee, which is responsible for evaluating submissions and awarding scholarships.

For renewal applicants, additional documentation may be required, including:

  • Proof of continued enrollment
  • An essay outlining academic progress

The program also verifies tribal enrollment status through the applicant’s respective tribal government.

Responsibilities of Award Recipients

Students who receive the scholarship are encouraged to:

  • Provide updates on their academic progress during the year
  • Maintain good standing in their educational institution
  • Submit renewal applications if seeking continued support

Additionally, recipients may have their names and photographs published by NMGC for promotional purposes, as part of the program’s outreach and impact visibility.

Why This Scholarship Matters

The NASP is more than just financial aid; it is an investment in the future of Native American communities. By supporting students across various educational pathways, including technical and vocational training, the program acknowledges the evolving demands of today’s workforce.

It empowers students not only to achieve academic success but also to return to their communities equipped with skills and knowledge that can drive development and innovation.

The Native American Scholarship Program is a powerful opportunity for eligible students to pursue their educational dreams without the burden of financial strain. Its inclusive design, flexible requirements, and commitment to community empowerment make it one of the most impactful scholarship programs available to Native American students in New Mexico.

If you meet the eligibility criteria, this is your chance to take a significant step toward achieving your academic and career goals.

Deadline : May 1 ,2026

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post Fully Fund Your Education: Native American Scholarship Program 2026–2027 by New Mexico Gas Company appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Program Coordinator Job in the Philippines (Remote) – Join The Max Foundation and Drive Global Health Equity
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
If you are passionate about healthcare delivery, patient advocacy, and global impact, this opportunity with The Max Foundation could be your next big step. With over 26 years of experience in advancing access to life-saving treatments, The Max Foundation is offering an entry-level Program Coordinator role based in Manila, Philippines (remote/home office), designed for individuals eager […]

The post Program Coordinator Job in the Philippines (Remote) – Join The Max Foundation and Drive Global Health Equity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about healthcare delivery, patient advocacy, and global impact, this opportunity with The Max Foundation could be your next big step. With over 26 years of experience in advancing access to life-saving treatments, The Max Foundation is offering an entry-level Program Coordinator role based in Manila, Philippines (remote/home office), designed for individuals eager to contribute to meaningful healthcare change.

About The Max Foundation

The Max Foundation is a globally recognized nonprofit organization committed to accelerating health equity. Operating across low- and middle-income countries, the organization has positively impacted more than 100,000 patients living with cancer and other critical illnesses. Their mission is rooted in the belief that geography should never determine access to life-saving medicines or quality healthcare.

Through partnerships with healthcare providers, patient organizations, and global stakeholders, The Max Foundation continues to develop scalable, patient-centered healthcare solutions that bring hope and dignity to underserved populations.

Role Overview: Program Coordinator

The Program Coordinator (PC) plays a vital role in managing the day-to-day operations of The Max Foundation’s programs in the Philippines. Reporting directly to the Program Officer, this contractor position involves a mix of administrative coordination, patient engagement, logistics management, and stakeholder collaboration.

This is not just an administrative role, it is a frontline position where you will directly impact patients’ lives by ensuring access to treatment, care, and support systems.

Key Responsibilities

As a Program Coordinator, you will be responsible for a wide range of duties that ensure smooth program implementation and patient support:

Program Operations & Administration

You will oversee the daily functioning of support programs, ensuring that all activities align with established organizational guidelines. This includes managing documentation, coordinating logistics, and maintaining operational efficiency.

Patient Support & Engagement

A critical part of your role involves working directly with patients. You will assist in enrolling eligible patients into programs, ensure compliance, and provide emotional support and basic disease education when necessary.

Collaboration with Healthcare Professionals

You will work closely with physicians, nurses, pharmacists, and other healthcare stakeholders. This collaboration ensures that patients receive timely access to medication and appropriate care.

Logistics & Supply Chain Coordination

The role includes supporting the importation and distribution of donated medicines. You will assist clinics in navigating customs clearance and ensuring that life-saving treatments reach patients without delay.

Data Management & Reporting

Maintaining accurate and up-to-date database records is essential. You will also report adverse events within 24 hours, ensuring compliance with safety and program protocols.

Advocacy & Storytelling

You will help amplify patient voices by collecting and sharing their stories. These narratives play a powerful role in advocacy and awareness campaigns.

Stakeholder Communication

Clear and professional communication with patients, healthcare professionals, and partner organizations is crucial. You will also provide regular updates to the Program Officer and support reporting processes.

Required Qualifications

To succeed in this role, candidates should meet the following criteria:

  • A diploma is required; a bachelor’s degree in Social Work, Public Health, Nursing, or a related field is preferred
  • At least one year of experience in a healthcare-related field
  • Experience in medicine management or supply chain is an added advantage
  • Strong communication skills to engage effectively with diverse stakeholders
  • Fluency in English and Filipino
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
  • Strong organizational and documentation skills
  • Ability to work independently and manage tasks efficiently
  • Willingness to work weekends when necessary
  • Must possess personal transportation

Skills and Attributes for Success

The ideal candidate is not just qualified but deeply aligned with The Max Foundation’s mission. You should demonstrate:

  • A strong passion for global health equity and patient-centered care
  • High attention to detail and commitment to quality work
  • Adaptability in a fast-paced, high-demand environment
  • Strong interpersonal and collaboration skills
  • A compassionate, people-first mindset
  • Resourcefulness and problem-solving ability

Work Structure and Compensation

This is a contractor position based in Manila, Philippines, operating from a home office. Compensation is competitive and commensurate with experience. Please note that relocation support is not provided.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply directly through The Max Foundation’s official careers page. Ensure that your application includes both a résumé and a well-crafted cover letter highlighting your experience and motivation.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This role is more than a job, it is an opportunity to become part of a global movement dedicated to saving lives and improving healthcare access. By joining The Max Foundation, you will contribute to a mission that transcends borders and transforms lives every day.

Deadline : Not specified , apply ASAP.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here. 

For more global opportunities, click here.

 

The post Program Coordinator Job in the Philippines (Remote) – Join The Max Foundation and Drive Global Health Equity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Grocers’ Charity Grant 2026: Funding Opportunity for UK Charities Supporting Communities, Health, and Social Impact
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Grocer’s Grant is a highly impactful funding opportunity designed to empower small UK-registered charities working to improve lives, strengthen communities, and address pressing social challenges. With an annual funding pool of approximately £1 million, this grant provides one-off financial support to charities that are making a tangible difference across a broad range of sectors. […]

The post Grocers’ Charity Grant 2026: Funding Opportunity for UK Charities Supporting Communities, Health, and Social Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Grocer’s Grant is a highly impactful funding opportunity designed to empower small UK-registered charities working to improve lives, strengthen communities, and address pressing social challenges. With an annual funding pool of approximately £1 million, this grant provides one-off financial support to charities that are making a tangible difference across a broad range of sectors.

If your organization is committed to social good; whether through supporting vulnerable populations, promoting health and well being, or preserving the environment; this grant offers a meaningful opportunity to secure funding and expand your impact.

Overview of the Grocers’ Charity Grant

The Grocers’ Charity provides one-off grants typically up to £5,000, although larger amounts may be awarded in exceptional cases where projects demonstrate significant need and potential impact. The funding is allocated at the discretion of the charity’s trustees, who carefully review each application to ensure alignment with the foundation’s mission and priorities.

The grant supports projects across several key areas of social intervention, ensuring a wide range of charitable initiatives can benefit.

Funding Focus Areas

The Grocers’ Charity Grant supports charities working in the following sectors:

1. Relief of Hardship

Projects that aim to alleviate poverty and improve living conditions are strongly encouraged. This includes initiatives providing training, improved facilities, and support for individuals at risk of homelessness or domestic abuse, particularly in high-deprivation areas.

2. Children and Young People (0–25 years)

Funding is available for programs that empower young people to reach their full potential. This includes initiatives focused on personal development, wellbeing, peer support, and group engagement activities.

3. The Elderly

Charities working to combat loneliness and social isolation among older adults are eligible. Programs may include befriending services, social inclusion initiatives, and support to enhance daily living.

4. Disability and Inclusion

Organizations that identify and remove barriers to participation for people with disabilities can apply. This includes frontline services that promote accessibility, inclusion, and equal opportunities.

5. Health

Health-focused charities can receive funding for ethical medical research, procurement of essential equipment, and initiatives supporting individuals with physical or mental health conditions. Note that health and medical charities may have a higher income threshold.

6. Military

Projects that support current and former service personnel and their families are eligible. This includes programs focused on employment, education, and overall wellbeing.

7. The Arts

The grant encourages artistic development and cultural engagement, especially for marginalized communities. This includes supporting artists with financial challenges or disabilities and promoting access to arts for underserved audiences.

8. Heritage

Funding is available for preserving historical assets, including restoring buildings (excluding places of worship) and conserving important objects or artworks.

9. Environment and Conservation

Charities working on environmental sustainability, biodiversity protection, and climate change mitigation can benefit. Projects may include habitat restoration, environmental education, and initiatives to reduce pollution and waste.

Grant Value and Financial Scope

  • Typical Grant Size: Up to £5,000
  • Exceptional Funding: Larger grants may be awarded for high-impact projects
  • Annual Funding Pool: Approximately £1 million

This funding can be used for tangible, project-based expenses that directly contribute to the charity’s mission and objectives.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the Grocers’ Charity Grant, applicants must:

  • Be a UK-registered charity
  • Have an annual turnover below £500,000
  • For health and medical charities, have an income below £15 million
  • Deliver projects that directly benefit communities within the UK

Restrictions

The following are not eligible to apply:

  • Places of worship
  • Educational institutions such as schools, colleges, and universities
  • Individuals
  • Non-UK registered organizations (including CICs)
  • Charities whose beneficiaries are located outside the UK
  • Charities exceeding the turnover threshold (except eligible medical charities)

Application Process

The application process is structured in two stages:

Stage 1: Initial Enquiry

Applicants must complete an online eligibility checklist and submit an Initial Enquiry Form. Responses are typically provided within three weeks after the application deadline.

Stage 2: Full Application (By Invitation Only)

Successful applicants from Stage 1 will be invited to complete a detailed online application form. They will have one week to submit this form.

Deadline

The closing date for applications is 1 September 2026. Applicants are advised to begin the process early to ensure adequate time for preparation and submission.

Why You Should Apply

The Grocers’ Charity Grant is more than just funding; it is an opportunity to scale your impact, strengthen your programs, and reach more beneficiaries. With its broad funding scope and commitment to community development, this grant is ideal for charities looking to make a measurable difference in society.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity , click here

For more global opportunities, click here

 

The post Grocers’ Charity Grant 2026: Funding Opportunity for UK Charities Supporting Communities, Health, and Social Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: MMD Grant Facility 2026: Call for Proposals Supporting Migration, Mobility, and Policy Innovation Across Africa and Europe
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The second Call for Proposals under the Migration and Mobility Dialogue (MMD) Grant Facility has officially been launched on April 7, 2026, offering a strategic funding opportunity for organizations working at the intersection of migration, policy, research, and international cooperation. Funded by the Directorate-General for International Partnerships (DG INTPA) under the Africa-EU Migration and Mobility […]

The post MMD Grant Facility 2026: Call for Proposals Supporting Migration, Mobility, and Policy Innovation Across Africa and Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The second Call for Proposals under the Migration and Mobility Dialogue (MMD) Grant Facility has officially been launched on April 7, 2026, offering a strategic funding opportunity for organizations working at the intersection of migration, policy, research, and international cooperation.

Funded by the Directorate-General for International Partnerships (DG INTPA) under the Africa-EU Migration and Mobility Dialogue Support Programme, this initiative aims to strengthen collaboration between African and European stakeholders while promoting evidence-based policy development and innovative solutions in migration governance.

Overview of the MMD Grant Facility

The MMD Grant Facility is designed to support short-term, high-impact projects that contribute to:

  • Strengthening migration and mobility dialogues
  • Enhancing policy coherence and cooperation
  • Generating research and knowledge outputs
  • Addressing emerging migration-related challenges

This second call builds on the success of previous initiatives and continues to promote cross-regional collaboration between Africa and Europe.

Funding Details

The total indicative budget allocated for this call is:

  • EUR 600,000

Individual grants will range between:

  • EUR 100,000 and EUR 150,000

Importantly, the program allows:

  • Up to 100% funding of eligible project costs, where properly justified

This makes the grant particularly accessible to non-profit organizations and research institutions with limited co-financing capacity.

Scope of Eligible Projects

Projects submitted under this call must align with the overarching goals of the MMD framework. Specifically, eligible actions should:

1. Support Migration Dialogues

Contribute to the operationalization of key migration frameworks, including:

  • Rabat Process
  • Khartoum Process
  • C2CMMD

2. Generate Knowledge and Policy Input

Projects should produce:

  • Research outputs
  • Policy recommendations
  • Data-driven insights

3. Align with Strategic Priorities

Actions must align with one or more domains of the:

  • Joint Valletta Action Plan

Or address transversal themes such as:

  • Climate-induced migration
  • Migration and gender
  • Migration and health

Geographic Scope and Implementation

Projects must:

  • Be implemented in up to three countries
  • Include at least one African Union Member State
  • Optionally include activities within the EU, particularly when targeting diaspora communities

Project Duration

  • Maximum duration: 6 months
  • All projects must be completed by: March 1, 2027

Who Can Apply?

The call is open to a wide range of non-profit and non-state actors, including:

  • Civil society organizations (CSOs)
  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • Diaspora organizations
  • Social enterprises
  • Universities and research centers

Lead Applicant Requirements

To be eligible, lead applicants must:

  • Be established for at least 2 years
  • Be registered in an African Union (AU) or European Union (EU) Member State

Partnership Requirements

  • Applications may include one co-applicant
  • EU-based lead applicants must include an AU-based co-applicant, unless they are diaspora organizations
  • AU-based applicants are not required to include co-applicants
  • Affiliated entities may also be included

Important Restriction

Organizations that received funding under the first MMD Call for Proposals (as lead or co-applicant) are not eligible for this round.

Application Process

All applications must be submitted via the official platform managed by the International Centre for Migration Policy Development.

  • Submission platform: ICMPD Electronic Application Platform
  • Each organization may participate in only one application (as lead, co-applicant, or affiliated entity)

Deadline

Application Deadline: May 1, 2026 at 17:00 (Vienna time)

Late submissions will not be considered.

Additional Information

Information Session

An online information session will be held on:

  • April 16, 2026 at 10:00 (Vienna time)

The registration link will be available on the application platform.

Questions and Clarifications

  • Questions can be submitted via the platform until: April 23, 2026

Annual Retrospective

Applicants are encouraged to review the 2025 Annual Retrospective, which highlights key achievements and impact of previously funded projects.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post MMD Grant Facility 2026: Call for Proposals Supporting Migration, Mobility, and Policy Innovation Across Africa and Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Becoming a Force of Nature 2026: Global Online Programme Empowering Young Change-Makers in Climate and Social Justice
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
In a rapidly changing world shaped by climate challenges and social inequalities, young people are increasingly stepping forward as catalysts for transformation. The Force of Nature Becoming a Force of Nature (BaFoN) Programme is a powerful global initiative designed to equip aspiring change makers with the clarity, skills, and confidence needed to turn their passion […]

The post Becoming a Force of Nature 2026: Global Online Programme Empowering Young Change-Makers in Climate and Social Justice appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
In a rapidly changing world shaped by climate challenges and social inequalities, young people are increasingly stepping forward as catalysts for transformation. The Force of Nature Becoming a Force of Nature (BaFoN) Programme is a powerful global initiative designed to equip aspiring change makers with the clarity, skills, and confidence needed to turn their passion into meaningful action.

This immersive online programme provides a structured yet deeply reflective journey, helping participants bridge the gap between their vision for a better world and the practical steps required to achieve it. If you are a young individual eager to create impact in climate or social justice spaces, this programme offers the tools and community you need to begin.

What is the Becoming a Force of Nature Programme?

Becoming a Force of Nature (BaFoN) is a three-part online programme tailored for individuals aged 16 to 35. It is delivered four times annually and focuses on guiding participants through a transformational process; from understanding their emotions and motivations to developing actionable solutions for real-world challenges.

The programme runs over three weeks, with one session per week. Each session lasts between 90 and 120 minutes, making it flexible and accessible for students, young professionals, and emerging leaders worldwide.

Participants are expected to dedicate approximately 2.5 hours per week, which includes live sessions and any reflective or follow-up activities. For those unable to attend live sessions, recordings are made available, ensuring inclusivity and convenience.

Programme Structure and Learning Journey

The BaFoN programme is thoughtfully divided into three interconnected workshops, each addressing a critical stage in the journey of becoming an effective changemaker.

1. Anxiety to Agency

This session explores the emotional and psychological dimensions of climate change and social issues. Participants learn to:

  • Understand the relationship between mental health and global challenges
  • Identify and acknowledge complex emotions such as anxiety, fear, or frustration
  • Build resilience strategies both individually and collectively
  • Apply practical tools to manage emotions and transform them into motivation for action

This stage is particularly important in helping participants move from feeling overwhelmed to feeling empowered.

2. Discovery (Agency to Efficacy)

In this phase, participants begin to uncover their unique identity as changemakers. The session focuses on:

  • Identifying personal strengths, passions, and talents
  • Clarifying individual values and purpose (“your why”)
  • Understanding what drives fulfillment and effectiveness in social impact work
  • Building a personal framework to guide decision-making and action

This stage ensures that participants are not only motivated but also aligned with their authentic selves.

3. Catalyst (Efficacy to Action)

The final session is action-oriented and equips participants with the tools to implement their ideas. Participants will:

  • Define and analyze a specific problem they want to address
  • Map out the problem with a focus on affected communities
  • Develop a clear and actionable plan
  • Identify next steps to turn ideas into tangible impact

By the end of this stage, participants leave with a concrete roadmap for creating change.

Programme Schedule

The upcoming May 2026 cohort will take place on:

  • 12th May 2026
  • 19th May 2026
  • 26th May 2026

Each session is conducted online, making it accessible to participants from all parts of the world.

Application Process and Deadline

To join the Becoming a Force of Nature programme, applicants are required to complete an online application form.

Application Deadline: 7th May 2026 at 12:00 PM BST

Due to the competitive nature of the programme and its global reach, early application is strongly encouraged.

Why You Should Apply

The Becoming a Force of Nature programme is not just another online course; it is a transformational experience. Participants gain:

  • A deeper understanding of their role in addressing global challenges
  • Practical tools for managing climate anxiety and building resilience
  • Clarity on personal purpose and direction
  • Skills to design and implement impactful projects
  • Access to a global network of like-minded young changemakers

In a time when the world needs bold, thoughtful, and action-driven leaders, this programme provides a strong foundation for anyone ready to step into that role.

The Becoming a Force of Nature programme stands out as a unique opportunity for young people to not only learn but also evolve. By combining emotional intelligence, self-discovery, and practical action planning, it ensures that participants are fully equipped to lead change in their communities and beyond.

If you are passionate about climate action, social justice, or community impact, this programme could be your next big step toward making a difference.

Application Deadline: 7th May 2026 at 12:00 PM BST

APPLY HERE

For mor information on this opportunity , click here.

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post Becoming a Force of Nature 2026: Global Online Programme Empowering Young Change-Makers in Climate and Social Justice appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Horizon Scanning for Emerging Technologies 2035: UK Government Survey for Early-Career Academics
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
As the global technological landscape evolves at an unprecedented pace, governments are increasingly relying on forward-looking research and expert insights to anticipate future disruptions. The Horizon Scanning for Emerging Technologies Survey, led by the UK Government Office for Science (GO-Science), represents a unique opportunity for early-career academics based in the United Kingdom to directly contribute […]

The post Horizon Scanning for Emerging Technologies 2035: UK Government Survey for Early-Career Academics appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
As the global technological landscape evolves at an unprecedented pace, governments are increasingly relying on forward-looking research and expert insights to anticipate future disruptions. The Horizon Scanning for Emerging Technologies Survey, led by the UK Government Office for Science (GO-Science), represents a unique opportunity for early-career academics based in the United Kingdom to directly contribute to shaping national policy and long-term strategic thinking.

This initiative is part of a broader effort to identify emerging technologies, potential risks, and transformative opportunities that could define the world by the year 2035.

What is Horizon Scanning and Why Does It Matter?

Horizon scanning is a strategic process used by governments and organizations to:

  • Detect early signals of change
  • Identify emerging technologies and trends
  • Assess future risks and opportunities
  • Support evidence-based policymaking

Through this survey, the UK Government aims to gather expert-driven insights from academics who are actively engaged in cutting-edge research and innovation.

About the Survey

The survey is conducted by the Emerging Technology Team within GO-Science, a body dedicated to analyzing technological developments and informing policy decisions at the highest levels of government.

Participants are invited to:

  • Assess up to three emerging technologies within their field
  • Share perspectives on potentially disruptive or unexpected innovations
  • Reflect on how these technologies could evolve and impact society by 2035

All questions are optional, allowing participants flexibility in how they contribute.

Why Participate?

This survey offers a valuable opportunity to:

  • Influence UK government policy and strategic planning
  • Contribute to national and global conversations on technology
  • Share your expertise on future innovations and disruptions
  • Participate in a confidential and anonymous research initiative

Your insights will play a critical role in shaping how governments prepare for technological change.

Participation Details

Participation in the survey is entirely voluntary. Respondents can:

  • Choose which questions to answer
  • Exit the survey at any time before submission
  • Submit responses only when comfortable

By completing and submitting the survey, participants confirm their consent to take part under the outlined conditions.

Data Privacy and Anonymity

A key feature of this initiative is its strong emphasis on privacy and ethical data handling:

  • The survey is designed not to collect personal data
  • Responses are fully anonymous
  • Participants are advised not to include identifiable information
  • Any accidental personal data will be removed before analysis

This ensures that all contributions remain secure and confidential.

How Your Responses Will Be Used

The collected data will be analyzed in aggregate to:

  • Identify key technological trends and themes
  • Support government briefings and internal analysis
  • Inform policy development and strategic decisions

Importantly:

  • Individual responses will not be attributed
  • Any external publications will present high-level, non-identifiable insights

Deadline

No specific deadline has been publicly stated. Participants are encouraged to complete the survey as soon as possible to ensure their insights are included in the analysis.

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Horizon Scanning for Emerging Technologies 2035: UK Government Survey for Early-Career Academics appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
